bull: Forward PE 13.4x is a 26% discount to the Industrials sector norm (18x), and analyst fair-value models (Simply Wall St) put fair value around DKK 231 vs. the current DKK 199 — roughly 16% upside to that anchor
Stochastic proxy at 1.7 and RSI at 39 mark a statistical extreme, historically a zone where oversold bounces occur
bear: Momentum is still negative on both the 5d (-0.85%) and 20d (-8.26%) windows and price sits below both SMA50 and SMA200 — the downtrend has not technically reversed yet
ROCK-B.CO.summary: Rockwool is deeply oversold (stoch 1.7) and trades at a meaningful discount to both its sector and analysts' own fair-value estimates, but the price trend is still pointing down with no confirmed reversal signal yet. This looks like a value/mean-reversion setup rather than a momentum trade.
ROCK-B.CO.key_risk: European construction/renovation demand stays weak long enough that the value discount turns out to be justified rather than a buying opportunity.

bull: Forward PE of 9.3x is a 58% discount to the Technology sector norm (22x) — among the cheapest names in the C25 universe
5-day momentum has turned positive (+3.5%) and price sits just above the 52-week low, a level that has so far acted as support
bear: Revenue is declining (-5.7% YoY) and price sits -17.2% below its SMA200, meaning the deep discount could reflect a genuine structural slowdown in IT-services demand rather than mispricing
No news catalyst is currently visible to trigger a re-rating
NNIT.CO.summary: NNIT is statistically the cheapest name in the deep-tier set, but the valuation discount is accompanied by declining revenue and a still-intact longer-term downtrend, making this a show-me value case rather than a conviction buy.
NNIT.CO.key_risk: A break below the 52-week low (36.3) would remove the one technical support level currently working in the stock's favor.

bull: FDA approval of the next-generation inflatable penile prosthesis expands Coloplast's addressable urology market
Forward PE of 15.9x is a 36% discount to the Healthcare sector norm (25x), while the 5.44% dividend yield provides income cushion
Positive momentum (+2.9% 5d, +11.7% 20d) with price back above SMA50
bear: RSI at 73.7 is approaching overbought territory, raising the odds of a near-term pullback after the recent run
Price remains -12.9% below SMA200, so the longer-term downtrend is only partially repaired
COLO-B.CO.summary: Coloplast combines a genuine product catalyst (FDA approval) with a valuation discount and a high dividend yield, and price action has turned constructive over the past month. The main near-term risk is that the rally has pushed RSI into stretched territory.
COLO-B.CO.key_risk: A technical pullback from overbought RSI levels, or a product/reimbursement setback that undercuts the FDA-approval catalyst.

bull: New large power-purchase agreement with Good Energy — described as the largest deal in Good Energy's history — signals continued commercial demand for Ørsted's output
Revenue grew +33% YoY and price now sits above SMA200 (+6.2%), suggesting the multi-year drawdown may be bottoming
Forward PE of 15.4x is a 23% discount to the Utilities sector norm
bear: Earnings collapsed -85% YoY, consistent with the risk of further offshore-wind impairments

Ørsted discontinued its credit-rating cooperation with S&P — while management maintains it targets solid creditworthiness, dropping a major rating agency relationship is a signal worth monitoring given the balance-sheet strain from the sector's cost overruns
ORSTED.CO.summary: Ørsted shows early signs of stabilizing — a new large offtake deal and a recovery above the 200-day average — after a brutal 54% drawdown from highs, but the earnings collapse and the unusual decision to end the S&P rating relationship keep the credibility of that recovery in question.
ORSTED.CO.key_risk: Another impairment charge on offshore-wind assets, or a market reaction to reduced credit-rating transparency, reigniting the downtrend.

bull: Earnings growth of +40% YoY is the strongest fundamental momentum in the deep-tier set, and an active share buy-back program is shrinking the share count
Forward PE of 12.7x is a 25% discount to the Consumer Defensive sector norm
bear: RSI at 86 and stochastic at 93 are extreme overbought readings — among the most stretched in the entire C25 universe

Momentum divergence of -14.2pp shows 5-day momentum already decelerating versus the 20-day trend, and Simply Wall St notes analyst price targets have not moved despite the rally, suggesting the market isn't yet pricing in further upside
RBREW.CO.summary: Royal Unibrew's underlying earnings growth and buyback support are genuinely strong, but the stock is technically as overbought as it gets in this universe and momentum is already cooling — the fundamental case is good, but the entry timing looks poor.
RBREW.CO.key_risk: A sharp mean-reversion pullback from the extreme overbought technical setup, independent of the fundamental picture.

bull: Forward PE (16.8x) is far below trailing PE (51.7x), implying the market expects a sharp earnings recovery
bear: RSI at 68.6 is approaching overbought while price is still -8% below SMA200, so the recovery is not yet confirmed
GN.CO.summary: GN Store Nord is pricing in a large earnings recovery but the technical trend hasn't fully turned yet.
GN.CO.key_risk: The assumed earnings recovery implied by the fwd/trailing PE gap fails to materialize.

bull: Strong uptrend with price near its 52-week high (-0.6%) and well above both SMA50 and SMA200
bear: RSI at 80.1 is firmly overbought, raising near-term pullback risk after the run
ISS.CO.summary: ISS is in a strong, well-established uptrend but the rally has pushed it into overbought territory.
ISS.CO.key_risk: A technical pullback from overbought RSI despite the constructive underlying trend.

bull: High 5.41% dividend yield with price holding modestly above both SMAs
bear: RSI at 77.5 is overbought and the screener score (38.7) shows limited edge versus stronger picks
TRYG.CO.summary: Tryg offers a steady, income-oriented uptrend but is technically stretched with little near-term edge.
TRYG.CO.key_risk: Overbought RSI reverting alongside a broader insurance-sector pullback.

bull: 20-day momentum has turned positive (+6.1%) after a deep -25% drawdown from highs
bear: Forward PE (18.3x) sits above trailing PE (12.1x), implying the market expects earnings to decline, and price remains below both SMAs
BAVA.CO.summary: Bavarian Nordic shows a tentative bounce but the earnings outlook implied by its valuation is deteriorating, not improving.
BAVA.CO.key_risk: Contract/pipeline disappointment confirming the earnings deceleration priced into the forward multiple.

bull: Strong, well-confirmed uptrend near 52-week highs (-2.9%) with forward PE below trailing PE, implying an improving earnings outlook
bear: Valuation is no longer cheap after the run, leaving less margin of safety
DEMANT.CO.summary: Demant is in a confirmed uptrend near its highs with an improving earnings outlook priced in.
DEMANT.CO.key_risk: A valuation-driven pause after the extended rally leaves little cushion for a fundamental miss.

bull: Cheap forward PE (10.0x) combined with a 4.11% dividend yield and a strong uptrend near 52-week highs
bear: RSI at 69.9 is nearing overbought, raising the risk of a near-term pause
ALSYDB.CO.summary: Sydbank pairs an attractive valuation and dividend with strong price action close to its highs.
ALSYDB.CO.key_risk: A regional-bank sector pullback or rate-driven repricing catching the stock near overbought levels.

bull: Sharp momentum recovery (+12.9% over 20 days) off a deep -36% drawdown from highs
bear: RSI at 83.6 is one of the most extreme overbought readings in the whole universe, and price remains -10% below SMA200 — this could be a bear-market rally rather than a trend reversal
AMBU-B.CO.summary: Ambu is rallying hard off a deep drawdown, but the move is technically extreme enough to raise real doubt about whether it's a genuine trend change.
AMBU-B.CO.key_risk: A sharp reversal of the extreme overbought bounce back into the prior downtrend.

bull: High 4.82% dividend yield with a steady uptrend and price above both SMAs
bear: Forward PE (15.3x) above trailing PE (12.0x) signals the market expects earnings growth to slow, and the stock is still -29% below its 52-week high
NOVO-B.CO.summary: Novo Nordisk is recovering steadily with an attractive dividend, but the market is pricing in decelerating earnings growth and the stock remains well off its highs.
NOVO-B.CO.key_risk: GLP-1 competitive pressure continuing to erode the earnings growth priced into forward estimates.

bull: Forward PE (19.3x) is dramatically below trailing PE (54.6x), implying the market expects a major earnings normalization
bear: RSI at 73.7 is overbought and the valuation is still not cheap on an absolute basis
DSV.CO.summary: DSV's valuation implies a large expected earnings recovery, but the stock is technically overbought after its run.
DSV.CO.key_risk: A pullback from overbought levels before the assumed earnings normalization is confirmed.

bull: Cheap forward PE (10.8x) with a strong uptrend near 52-week highs (-0.8%)
bear: RSI at 79.3 is overbought, raising near-term pullback risk
JYSK.CO.summary: Jyske Bank combines an attractive valuation with strong price action, despite being technically stretched.
JYSK.CO.key_risk: Overbought RSI triggering a short-term pullback despite the favorable valuation backdrop.

bull: Forward PE (13.8x) offers some valuation support if the downtrend stabilizes
bear: 20-day momentum is sharply negative (-10.3%) and price sits below both SMA50 and SMA200 — a clear, accelerating downtrend
FLS.CO.summary: FLSmidth is in a clear and accelerating downtrend with no technical signs of stabilization yet.
FLS.CO.key_risk: Continued weakness in mining/cement capex spending extending the downtrend further.

bull: 20-day momentum is solidly positive (+11.6%) with price back above SMA50
bear: 5-day momentum has turned negative (-3.0%) and price is still slightly below SMA200
GMAB.CO.summary: Genmab's recovery over the past month is real but has stalled in the last few sessions.
GMAB.CO.key_risk: The recent 5-day pullback extending into a fuller reversal of the 20-day recovery.

bull: Cheap forward PE (11.3x), a 4.61% dividend yield, and a strong long-term uptrend (+21% above SMA200) near 52-week highs
bear: Minor 5-day pullback (-0.7%) and a relatively low screener score versus higher-conviction picks
DANSKE.CO.summary: Danske Bank shows an attractive combination of cheap valuation, income, and a well-established uptrend near its highs.
DANSKE.CO.key_risk: A broader Nordic banking-sector pullback interrupting the long-term uptrend.

bull: Strong single-day bounce (+2.4%) with price still above SMA200, keeping the longer-term uptrend intact
bear: 20-day momentum is negative (-1.3%) and price is essentially flat versus SMA50 — choppy, indecisive action
VWS.CO.summary: Vestas is showing a bounce within an otherwise indecisive, range-bound trend.
VWS.CO.key_risk: The choppy price action resolving to the downside, breaking the SMA200 support that has held so far.

bull: Very high 7.66% dividend yield with a steady uptrend near 52-week highs (-0.9%)
bear: RSI at 65.2 is approaching overbought and the screener score (20.2) is among the lowest in the universe, signaling limited near-term edge
NDA-DK.CO.summary: Nordea offers a standout dividend yield within a steady uptrend, though the screener sees limited additional edge here.
NDA-DK.CO.key_risk: A rate- or sector-driven pullback eroding the income case despite the high yield.

bull: Reasonable forward PE (13.1x) with a steady uptrend and 3.13% dividend yield
bear: RSI at 69.5 is approaching overbought and the low screener score (15.6) suggests limited relative edge
CARL-B.CO.summary: Carlsberg is trending steadily with reasonable valuation support but shows little standout edge versus peers.
CARL-B.CO.key_risk: A stretched RSI reverting into a pullback with no strong catalyst to offset it.

bull: Powerful 20-day rally (+21.4%) has pushed price well above both SMA50 (+18.6%) and SMA200 (+25.3%)
bear: Forward PE (18.9x) sits well above trailing PE (11.7x), implying the market expects earnings deceleration, and 5-day momentum has already turned slightly negative
PNDORA.CO.summary: Pandora's rally has been powerful, but the valuation gap between forward and trailing earnings suggests the market is now pricing in a slowdown, and momentum is already cooling.
PNDORA.CO.key_risk: The implied earnings deceleration being confirmed, ending the recent rally.

bull: Steady uptrend with price above both SMAs and a 2.83% dividend yield
bear: Negative forward PE signals expected losses/high earnings uncertainty, and freight-rate cyclicality is a persistent risk
MAERSK-B.CO.summary: Maersk B is trending steadily but forward earnings visibility is poor, consistent with ongoing freight-rate cyclicality.
MAERSK-B.CO.key_risk: A freight-rate downturn confirming the earnings uncertainty implied by the negative forward PE.

bull: Same steady uptrend and dividend support as the B-shares, with price above both SMAs
bear: Forward PE is unavailable (earnings uncertainty) and the A-shares are thinner-traded than the B-shares
MAERSK-A.CO.summary: Maersk A mirrors the B-share picture — a steady uptrend clouded by poor forward earnings visibility.
MAERSK-A.CO.key_risk: Freight-rate cyclicality and the lower liquidity of the A-share line versus the B-share line.

bull: Price is near its 52-week high (-6.2%) and forward PE (24.0x) implies earnings roughly doubling versus trailing (44.2x)
bear: Even on the improved forward basis, the valuation is still rich relative to C25 peers, and the screener score (9.0) is the lowest in the universe
NSIS-B.CO.summary: Novonesis trades near its highs on an improving earnings outlook, but remains relatively expensive with the weakest screener score in the universe.
NSIS-B.CO.key_risk: The assumed earnings improvement disappointing, leaving the stock trading on an unsupported high multiple.
